transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: BusinessNetflix Will Acquire Warner Bros
SOURCE: Forbes

ARTICLE:

BreakingBusinessNetflix Will Acquire Warner Bros. In $83 Billion Deal—Discovery Will Be Split OffBySiladitya Ray,Forbes Staff. Siladitya Ray is a New Delhi-based Forbes news team reporter.Follow AuthorDec 05, 2025, 07:52am ESTDec 05, 2025, 08:53am ESTShareSaveCommentToplineNetflix on Friday announced it had reached a deal to acquire Warner Bros.— including the company's film and television studios and streamer HBO Max—for $82.7 billion, in move that follows a weeks-long bidding war in which the streaming giant beat out Paramount and Comcast.Warner Bros. Discovery has reportedly entered exclusive talks with Netflix for a sale of its studio and TV businesses.Anadolu via Getty ImagesKey FactsIn a press statement the company said the cash and stock deal is valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share and it will close after the media giant's TV network business is spun off into a separate public company, Discovery Global.The deal is subject to regulatory approvals and is expected to be completed in the third quarter of 2026.If it goes through, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash per share and $4.501 worth of shares of Netflix's stock.According to Deadline and CNN, Paramount Skydance had offered $27 per share for the entire Warner Bros. Discovery business, including its cable channels like CNN and TNT. Comcast also made a bid only for Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming businesses.Bloomberg previously reported that Netflix is offering Warner Bros. Discovery a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators don't clear the deal.
---

{
  "summary": "Netflix has announced a definitive agreement to acquire Warner Bros. in a transaction valued at $82.7 billion. The cash and stock transaction, priced at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) share, follows a competitive bidding process against other major entertainment companies, including Paramount and Comcast. As part of the deal, WBD's television network business will be spun off into a separate public entity called Discovery Global prior to closing. If the acquisition secures regulatory approval and is finalized as planned in the third quarter of 2026, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash and $4.501 in Netflix shares per share held. Competing bids included a $27 per share offer from Paramount Skydance and a separate offer from Comcast for WBD's studio and streaming divisions. Netflix has included a $5 billion breakup fee in the agreement, payable if regulatory authorities do not approve the transaction.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Netflix will acquire Warner Bros., including HBO Max and its studios, for $82.7 billion.",
    "The transaction is valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share with a mix of cash and stock.",
    "Discovery's TV network business will be separated and listed as Discovery Global before the deal's closing.",
    "Regulatory approval is required, with a target deal completion in the third quarter of 2026.",
    "Netflix has offered a $5 billion breakup fee if the deal is blocked by regulators."
  ],
  "sources_represented": ["Forbes", "Deadline", "CNN", "Bloomberg"]
}

raw_response:

  "summary": "Netflix has announced a definitive agreement to acquire Warner Bros. in a transaction valued at $82.7 billion. The cash and stock transaction, priced at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) share, follows a competitive bidding process against other major entertainment companies, including Paramount and Comcast. As part of the deal, WBD's television network business will be spun off into a separate public entity called Discovery Global prior to closing. If the acquisition secures regulatory approval and is finalized as planned in the third quarter of 2026, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash and $4.501 in Netflix shares per share held. Competing bids included a $27 per share offer from Paramount Skydance and a separate offer from Comcast for WBD's studio and streaming divisions. Netflix has included a $5 billion breakup fee in the agreement, payable if regulatory authorities do not approve the transaction.",

  ],

}

parsed_output.summary: Netflix has announced a definitive agreement to acquire Warner Bros. in a transaction valued at $82.7 billion. The cash and stock transaction, priced at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) share, follows a competitive bidding process against other major entertainment companies, including Paramount and Comcast. As part of the deal, WBD's television network business will be spun off into a separate public entity called Discovery Global prior to closing. If the acquisition secures regulatory approval and is finalized as planned in the third quarter of 2026, each WBD shareholder will receive $23.25 in cash and $4.501 in Netflix shares per share held. Competing bids included a $27 per share offer from Paramount Skydance and a separate offer from Comcast for WBD's studio and streaming divisions. Netflix has included a $5 billion breakup fee in the agreement, payable if regulatory authorities do not approve the transaction.
key_facts: Netflix will acquire Warner Bros., including HBO Max and its studios, for $82.7 billion.
The transaction is valued at $27.75 per Warner Bros. Discovery share with a mix of cash and stock.
Discovery's TV network business will be separated and listed as Discovery Global before the deal's closing.
Regulatory approval is required, with a target deal completion in the third quarter of 2026.
Netflix has offered a $5 billion breakup fee if the deal is blocked by regulators.
